summary: A multi-story residential or administrative building with a distinctive cross-shaped floor plan. The structure features 4 visible floors with a brick and stone exterior, multiple small windows, and a crenellated brick roof. A central cross monument or tower element dominates the roofline. The building is surrounded by a white fence perimeter on a dirt foundation.
building_type: residential or administrative building with religious/ceremonial cross monument

shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along both axes, with central cross alignment
shape.roof_type: crenellated brick with central cross tower

elements: multiple floors with horizontal stone bands
rebuild_hints: Build cross-shaped footprint with 4 equal arms extending from center
Construct 4 floors with stonebrick walls and regular window placement
Add horizontal stone slab bands between floors for visual separation
Build crenellated brick roof around perimeter
Construct tall central cross monument on roof using brick, with decorative top
Place white fence blocks around entire perimeter at ground level
Ensure bilateral symmetry along both N-S and E-W axes
Add corner support pillars at cross intersections
Use glass for small window openings on all visible sides
uncertainties: Exact window dimensions and spacing pattern - appears regular but precise count unclear from angles
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Exact height of central cross tower relative to building
Whether structure extends below visible ground level
Specific stone/brick variant used (standard vs mossy vs cracked variants)
Presence of doors or entrances on non-front faces
Roof material composition beyond visible crenellations
